message:
Add WASM build gating, dependency fencing, and compile_error guards

Introduce the `wasm-browser-preview` feature flag and restructure the
build to reject unsupported feature combinations at compile time when
targeting wasm32.

Cargo.toml:
- Add `wasm-browser-preview = []` feature to [features] table
- Move native-only dependencies (polling, socket2, libc, nix,
  signal-hook, signal-hook-registry) to a new
  `[target.'cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))'.dependencies]` section
  so they are never pulled into browser builds
- Register `wasm-browser-preview` and `simd-intrinsics` in the
  check-cfg `unexpected_cfgs` lints list

src/lib.rs:
- Add compile_error! when wasm32 is targeted without the
  wasm-browser-preview feature enabled, providing a clear message
  that arbitrary wasm32 compilation is not supported
- Add compile_error! for nine feature flags that are categorically
  incompatible with wasm32 (cli, io-uring, tls, tls-native-roots,
  tls-webpki-roots, sqlite, postgres, mysql, kafka) with per-feature
  diagnostic messages
- Reorganize all module declarations into clearly delineated sections:
  portable modules, feature-gated modules (cli, database, tls),
  platform-specific modules gated with cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))
  (fs, grpc, messaging, server, signal), and test-only modules

src/http/h1/mod.rs:
- Gate the `listener` and `server` submodules and their re-exports
  behind cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32")) since accept loops and
  TCP listeners have no browser equivalent

docs/integration.md:
- Document the wasm32 guardrail contract: required feature flag,
  rejected feature list, and pointer to the WASM browser plan

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -32,6 +32,9 @@ include = [
 
 [features]
 default = ["test-internals"]
+# Explicit opt-in for experimental browser-targeted builds.
+# This does not imply full wasm parity; it only enables guarded compilation paths.
+wasm-browser-preview = []
 # Enable internal test helpers for integration tests.
 # This exposes private APIs like Cx::new() and is NOT for production use.
 test-internals = ["dep:tracing", "dep:tracing-subscriber", "dep:visibility"]
@@ -94,17 +97,13 @@ required-features = ["cli"]
 thiserror = "2.0"
 crossbeam-queue = "0.3"
 parking_lot = "0.12"
-polling = "3.11"
 slab = "0.4"
 smallvec = "1.15"
 pin-project = "1.1"
 rmp-serde = "1.3"
 serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
 serde_json = "1.0"
 memchr = "2.8"
-socket2 = { version = "0.6", features = ["all"] }
-libc = "0.2"
-nix = { version = "0.31", features = ["fs", "poll", "socket", "uio", "user"] }
 getrandom = "0.4"
 # Use maintained bincode-next (crate imported as `bincode`) with serde interop.
 bincode = { package = "bincode-next", version = "2.1", features = ["serde"] }
@@ -144,8 +143,7 @@ rustls-pemfile = { version = "2.2", optional = true }
 base64 = "0.22.1"
 # SHA-1 hashing for WebSocket Sec-WebSocket-Accept calculation (RFC 6455)
 sha1 = "0.10"
-# Cross-platform signal registration (safe wrapper around OS signal handlers)
-signal-hook = "0.3"
+# signal-hook moved to [target.'cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))'.dependencies]
 # X.509 certificate parsing for SPKI extraction
 x509-parser = { version = "0.18", optional = true }
 # Optional TOML config file support for RuntimeBuilder
@@ -175,8 +173,15 @@ loom = { version = "0.7", optional = true }
 [target.'cfg(target_os = "linux")'.dependencies]
 io-uring = { version = "0.7.11", optional = true }
 
-# Rustls crypto provider - ring is the most widely used
+# Native-only dependencies: platform I/O, sockets, signals, and crypto.
+# These are excluded from wasm32 browser builds (umelq.3.3).
 [target.'cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))'.dependencies]
+polling = "3.11"
+socket2 = { version = "0.6", features = ["all"] }
+libc = "0.2"
+nix = { version = "0.31", features = ["fs", "poll", "socket", "uio", "user"] }
+signal-hook = "0.3"
+# Rustls crypto provider - ring is the most widely used
 ring = { version = "0.17", optional = true }
 
 [dev-dependencies]
@@ -269,7 +274,7 @@ codegen-units = 1
 [lints.rust]
 # Default to deny for unsafe code - specific modules that require it can use #[allow(unsafe_code)]
 # The epoll reactor requires unsafe for the polling crate's FFI operations
-unexpected_cfgs = { level = "warn", check-cfg = ['cfg(loom)', 'cfg(feature, values("quic", "http3"))'] }
+unexpected_cfgs = { level = "warn", check-cfg = ['cfg(loom)', 'cfg(feature, values("quic", "http3", "wasm-browser-preview", "simd-intrinsics"))'] }
 unsafe_code = "deny"
 missing_docs = "warn"
 unused = { level = "warn", priority = -1 }

@@ -152,6 +152,26 @@ Override via `RuntimeBuilder::obligation_leak_response(...)` or
 See `TESTING.md` for test categories, logging conventions, conformance suite usage,
 and fuzzing instructions.
 
+### wasm32 Guardrails
+
+Browser-targeted compilation is explicitly gated to prevent accidental partial
+builds with semantic holes:
+
+- `target_arch = "wasm32"` requires `--features wasm-browser-preview`.
+- The following features are compile-time rejected on wasm32:
+  - `cli`
+  - `io-uring`
+  - `tls`
+  - `tls-native-roots`
+  - `tls-webpki-roots`
+  - `sqlite`
+  - `postgres`
+  - `mysql`
+  - `kafka`
+
+Policy and deterministic dependency-audit profiles are documented in
+`docs/wasm_dependency_audit_policy.md`.
+
 ### Examples
 
 Examples live in `examples/` and cover:
